definition: a lesson about right and wrong that a story teaches you

definition: about dogs or like a dog
blanked: The vet is a ______ expert who treats dogs.

definition: to begin to get something
blanked: After a few tries, most kids ______ to the card game.

definition: the thin covering around a living building block
blanked: The ______ controls what goes in and out of the cell.

definition: to be decided by one main thing
blanked: In the end, the choice will ______ which flavor you like best.

definition: to travel each day between home and work or school
blanked: She ______ by bus every morning.

definition: a plant grown in large amounts for food
blanked: Corn is an important ______ grown by many farmers.

definition: to move going down
blanked: The hikers ______ the mountain before sunset.

definition: at risk of dying out; close to becoming extinct
blanked: The panda is an ______ species that needs protection.

definition: to turn from liquid into a gas because of heat
blanked: The puddle will ______ in the hot sun.

definition: no longer living anywhere on Earth
blanked: Dinosaurs became ______ millions of years ago.

definition: to not keep up with others
blanked: If you miss class, you might ______ on homework.

definition: a phrase that means something different from the actual words
blanked: Raining cats and dogs is a ______ meaning heavy rain.

definition: the order in which living things eat each other
blanked: In a ______, grass feeds rabbits, and rabbits feed hawks.

definition: many food chains linked together in nature
blanked: The ______ shows how all animals in the pond depend on each other.

definition: to manage with just enough
blanked: She can ______ with only a small lunch if she eats a big breakfast.

definition: to agree or to work together with
blanked: He decided to ______ his friend's idea.

definition: caused by the force that pulls things toward Earth
blanked: ______ pull keeps our feet on the ground.

definition: the way gases trap heat around the Earth
blanked: The ______ keeps Earth warm enough for life.

definition: to sleep deeply through the winter
blanked: Bears ______ in caves during the cold winter months.

definition: to keep heat or cold from passing through
blanked: Thick walls ______ the house from the winter cold.

definition: to pay attention to and record something
blanked: ______ how much water you drink each day.

definition: a shape on the ground, like a hill or valley
blanked: Mountains and valleys are types of ______.

definition: the stages a living thing goes through from when a baby comes into the world to death
blanked: The ______ of a frog starts as an egg.

definition: to watch carefully to protect
blanked: Big sisters ______ their little brothers.

definition: found in or connected to the sea
blanked: ______ animals like dolphins live in the ocean.

definition: the babies or young of an animal
blanked: The mother duck swam with her ______ behind her.

definition: because of; as a result of
blanked: School was closed ______ the heavy snow.

definition: suddenly and without warning
blanked: ______, a rainbow appeared in the sky.

definition: a special form of oxygen high in the sky that blocks harmful rays from the sun
blanked: The ______ layer protects us from the sun's strong rays.

definition: tiny living things floating in the ocean
blanked: Whales eat huge amounts of ______ every day.

definition: the way someone sees or thinks about something
blanked: The story is told from the dog's ______.

definition: something that makes air, water, or land dirty
blanked: Smoke from factories is a ______ that harms the air.

definition: lasting a long time
blanked: A ______ drought dried up the river.

definition: to suggest an idea or plan
blanked: He ______ a plan to clean up the park.

definition: to make more of the same kind
blanked: Plants ______ by making seeds.

definition: able to be dissolved in liquid
blanked: Sugar is ______ in water.

definition: caused by or coming from a mountain that pushes out hot rock
blanked: The ______ island was formed by an eruption long ago.

definition: how water moves from Earth to sky and back again
blanked: The ______ includes drying up, clouds, and rain.

definition: an area of land where all water drains to one river
blanked: Our town is part of the river's ______.

definition: to end up in a place or situation
blanked: If you don't follow the map, you might ______ lost.

definition: how sound moves in a room
blanked: The concert hall had perfect ______ for music.

definition: a metal made by mixing two metals together
blanked: Bronze is an ______ of copper and tin.

definition: to gather a large amount
blanked: She ______ a big collection of seashells.

definition: a special song for a country or group
blanked: Everyone stood for the national ______ before the game.

definition: an opening or hole, mainly in a thing that takes pictures
blanked: He widened the ______ on the camera to let in more light.

definition: a pleasant smell
blanked: The ______ of fresh bread filled the bakery.

definition: to say yes and agree with what someone asks or suggests
blanked: Everyone ______ to the new reading schedule.

definition: to show that something is true
blanked: Her good grades ______ to her hard work.

definition: to stop and refuse to go on
blanked: The horse ______ at the fence and would not jump.

definition: to enjoy a warm feeling and light
blanked: The lizard ______ on the warm sunny rock.

definition: the solid rock under the soil
blanked: Builders dug down to the ______ before laying the foundation.

definition: to give as a gift or honor
blanked: The queen ______ a medal on the brave firefighter.

definition: a soft, wet area of land
blanked: Their boots sank into the muddy ______.

definition: the study of plants
blanked: He studied ______ to learn about different flowers.

definition: a short period of something, often illness or action
blanked: He had a ______ of sneezing from the dusty room.

definition: water that is a mix of salt water and fresh water
blanked: The ______ water near the river mouth tasted slightly salty.

definition: a short, stiff hair or fiber
blanked: The paintbrush had soft ______.

definition: the main force of something
blanked: The coast bore the ______ of the hurricane.

definition: to move or speak in a clumsy way
blanked: He ______ through his speech, but everyone still clapped.

definition: a very tiny blood vessel
blanked: A ______ is so tiny that you cannot see it without a microscope.

definition: a deep crack or gap in the earth
blanked: They peered over the edge of the deep ______.

definition: a small group of things close together
blanked: A ______ of mushrooms grew near the old tree.

definition: old plant parts that have broken down and feed the soil
blanked: We put banana peels in the ______ bin.

definition: curved toward the inside like the inside of a bowl
blanked: The ______ mirror made her face look tiny.

definition: curved toward the outside like the outside of a ball
blanked: The ______ lens made the letters look bigger.

definition: a narrow crack in a rock or wall
blanked: A tiny plant grew out of a ______ in the cliff.

definition: something used to trick or attract
blanked: The hunter placed a duck ______ on the lake.

definition: a longer path taken to avoid something
blanked: We took a ______ around the flooded road.

definition: to make a liquid weaker by adding water
blanked: She ______ the strong juice with water.

definition: to become smaller or fewer
blanked: The candle flame ______ until it went out.

definition: a small round current of water or air
blanked: Leaves spun in an ______ at the edge of the stream.

definition: to surround and cover completely
blanked: Fog ______ the entire valley overnight.

definition: to free from a difficult or tangled situation
blanked: She ______ the kite from the tree branches.

definition: a sudden strong burst of wind
blanked: A ______ of wind blew the hat off his head.

definition: to change shape smoothly
blanked: The clay ______ into a beautiful pot on the wheel.

definition: to scrub hard or search thoroughly
blanked: He ______ the beach for sea glass.

definition: fine sand or clay put down by water
blanked: ______ from the flood made the soil very rich.

definition: a tiny part that can grow into a new plant or a living thing like a mushroom
blanked: Mushrooms spread by releasing ______ into the air.

definition: to go under the surface of water
blanked: The submarine ______ below the waves.

definition: to dig up or discover something hidden
blanked: Workers ______ old coins while digging the foundation.

definition: green with growing plants
blanked: The ______ hillside was covered in lush grass.

definition: a young tree
blanked: We planted a ______ in the schoolyard on Earth Day.

definition: the top layer of soil where plants grow
blanked: The farmer added rich ______ to the garden bed.
